source: AD&D 2nd Edition Complete Psionicist's Handbook (PHBR5), Complete Shaman's Handbook (PHBR12), Al-Qadim Campaign Setting, Tome of Magic, Complete Priest's Handbook (PHBR3)
description: Additional character classes beyond the PHB. These classes are available in specific settings or as optional expansions.

psionicist.description: A dedicated practitioner of mental power. Masters of psionics drawn from the Complete Psionicist's Handbook.
psionicist.armor: None (robes, normal clothes; no metal armor — interferes with psionic focus)
psionicist.weapons: Any one-handed weapon, no two-handed melee
psionicist.psp_gain_per_level: See psionics file for per-level gain

disciplines_learned.sciences_per_level: Gain 1 science per level from known disciplines
disciplines_learned.devotions_per_level: Gain 2 devotions per level from known disciplines
disciplines_learned.additional_disciplines: At levels 5, 10, 15, 20 may add new discipline study

special_abilities_by_level.level_1: Psionic Combat modes (1 attack, 1 defense); all attack modes and defenses available at full PSP cost
special_abilities_by_level.level_3: Psionic defense modes improve; access to attack modes beyond one
special_abilities_by_level.level_5: Can learn 3rd discipline; Inner Strength (recover 2 PSPs/hour instead of 1 while active)
special_abilities_by_level.level_7: Power Score bonus: all power scores improve by +1
special_abilities_by_level.level_9: Mind Mastery: maintain two powers simultaneously without penalty
special_abilities_by_level.level_11: Power Score bonus improves by another +1
special_abilities_by_level.level_15: Grand access to all disciplines at reduced PSP cost (-10% to all costs)

special_rules: Cannot use psionic powers while in metal armor
Cannot multi-class with psionicist in most settings
Psionicist/fighter or psionicist/thief multi-class allowed in some settings
Wild talents in non-psionicists are inferior versions of standard powers
Psionicist vs. psionicist combat uses special psionic combat rules

shaman.description: Spirit-world intercessors who draw power from ancestor spirits and nature spirits. Different from clerics in that their power comes from negotiation with spirits rather than divine bestowal.
shaman.armor: Hide, leather, ring, studded leather; no metal armor; shields allowed
shaman.weapons: Any one-handed non-edged weapon; spear; staff; club; hammer; axe; bow
shaman.spell_source: Spirit world — spirits grant spells rather than deity

spells_per_day.description: Same progression as cleric for 1st-7th level spells
spells_per_day.note: Must spend time at Spirit World threshold each morning to regain spells

special_abilities: Spirit Sight: see spirit world and spirits at all times; can communicate with them
Totemic Bond: bond with one animal totem at 1st level; gain minor totem abilities
Trance: enter spirit world trance once/day; gain information from spirits (as Contact spell)
Turn undead as cleric of same level (using spirits rather than divine power)
Create and use spirit fetishes (binding spirits into objects for later use)
Speak with animals related to totem freely (no spell required)
Spirit Armor: once/day, call on protective spirits for +2 AC for 1 round/level

totem_animals.description: Each shaman bonds with a totem animal at 1st level

examples.benefits: +2 STR when fighting defensively; Hibernate once per year (as power)
Eagle Eye (as spell) 3/day; +2 to surprise opponents outdoors
Tracking at +2; run at +3 movement for 1 turn/day
+4 to saves vs. poison; Charm Reptiles 1/day
Understand omens; +2 to divination checks; speak with birds
+2 AC from natural toughness; ignore movement penalties in water
+3 movement on open terrain; never thrown from mount

restrictions: Must maintain relationship with spirit world: daily ritual 1 hour
Cannot use edged metal weapons (offends nature spirits)
If shaman betrays totem or spirits: lose all abilities until reconciled

witch_doctor.description: Tribal healers and cursers who combine aspects of wizard and shaman. Rare class.
special_abilities: Combines priest and wizard spellcasting (learns spells from both lists at reduced rate)
Curse magic: can place minor curses without spell use (1/day)
Brew potions and create magical fetishes
Identify magic items as Identify (1/day)
Speak with dead (as spell) 3/week
Control undead as if same-level cleric (but weakly)
witch_doctor.spells: Access to 1st through 6th level spells from both cleric and wizard lists; learns spells as per wizard (via study) AND via prayer each morning

shair.description: Al-Qadim wizard-priests who use a magical familiar (Gen) to fetch spells from the Plane of Elemental Fire. Unique to the Al-Qadim setting.
shair.weapons: As standard mage (staff, daggers, darts, slings)

special_ability_gen.description: The Gen is a tiny elemental being (4 inch tall fire creature) who lives in a ring worn by the Sha'ir
abilities: Travel to the Plane of Elemental Fire

spell_acquisition.method: Send Gen to Elemental Plane to fetch spells rather than memorizing them
spell_acquisition.time: Gen returns in 1d6 turns; 1 day if spell is very high level
spell_acquisition.chance_of_success: 95% for spell 3+ levels below Sha'ir level; -5% per level above
spell_acquisition.concurrent_spells: Can request 1 spell per level per day; Gen can carry maximum 2 spells
spell_acquisition.casting: Once fetched, spell can be cast at will until cast; then Gen must fetch again
spell_acquisition.advantages: Can potentially access any arcane spell; not limited to prepared spell selection
spell_acquisition.disadvantages: Time delay; Gen can be harmed, forcing longer fetch times

special_abilities: Elemental speak: converse with any elemental being freely
Elemental detect: know direction and distance to nearest elemental being within 100 ft
Gate (elemental): once per week, open gate to appropriate elemental plane
Fire walk (at 7th level): immune to fire; walk through flames unharmed

spell_progression.description: Can eventually use spells up to 9th level
spell_progression.note: Sha'ir can fetch priest spells too, but Gen returns in 1 extra turn for priest spells

specialty_priests.description: Specialty priests serve specific deities and receive unique powers reflecting their deity's portfolio. From the Complete Priest's Handbook.

creation_rules.description: DMs can create specialty priests using the sphere system and powers from the CPH
steps: 2. Select major spheres (full access) aligned with portfolio
3. Select minor spheres (lower-level access) for secondary portfolio areas
4. Assign 1-4 special powers from the power list
5. Determine turn undead ability (may be replaced by other power)
6. Set weapon and armor restrictions appropriate to deity

priest_of_war.description: Serves deities of war, strategy, and battle
special_abilities: Battle Fury 1/day: +2 to hit, +2 damage, +2 saves for 5 rounds
Know Enemy (as spell) 1/day
Inspire troops: +1 morale to 50 allied troops within 30 ft
Cannot be flanked (always alert in battle)

priest_of_nature.description: Serves deities of nature, wilderness, and the natural world
priest_of_nature.weapons: Non-edged: staff, sling, club; also spear
special_abilities: Pass Without Trace (always active outdoors)
Identify plants and animals (free, always)
Speak with animals/plants (no spell required)
Wilderness Sense: never lost outdoors; always know direction/weather

priest_of_knowledge.description: Serves deities of knowledge, lore, and magic
special_abilities: Instant recall: never forget anything read or heard
Identify (as spell) 3/day
Legend Lore (as spell) 1/week
Read magic (always active)
+2 to all INT-based proficiency checks

priest_of_death.description: Serves deities of death, fate, and the underworld
priest_of_death.turn_undead: Commanded (not turned): controls undead instead of destroying
special_abilities: Death touch 1/day: touch attack; target saves vs. death magic at -2 or dies
Speak with dead (no spell required) 1/day
Undead are neutral to priest (do not attack unless ordered to)
Age target 1d10 years (touch, save vs. spell) 1/day

priest_of_trickery.description: Serves deities of deception, cunning, and thieves
priest_of_trickery.special_substitute: Thieve's cant and street sense free
priest_of_trickery.weapons: Short blade, dagger, sling, short bow
special_abilities: Non-detection (always active; spells cannot scry priest)
Ventriloquism (no spell required) 3/day
Disguise (as proficiency) with +5 bonus
Pick pockets as 3rd-level thief

sphere_system.description: The complete priest sphere system from the CPH

spheres.name: All
spheres.description: Spells common to all priests: Cure Light Wounds, Detect Magic, etc.

spheres.name: Charm
spheres.description: Magic to influence minds and reactions

spheres.name: Creation
spheres.description: Spells that create matter or food

spheres.name: Elemental
spheres.description: Spells related to the four elements

spheres.name: Sun
spheres.description: Light, radiance, and the power of day

crusader.description: A holy warrior variant from the Complete Priest's Handbook; different from Paladin in that Crusaders serve any deity's cause without the strict code.
crusader.spells: Priest spells 1st through 5th level (limited compared to full priest)
special_abilities: Fight as warrior (THAC0)
Cast priest spells through 5th level
Turn undead as cleric 2 levels lower
Lay on hands once per day (as paladin but scaled to deity's alignment)
Immune to fear when fighting in name of deity
+1 to attack and damage vs. deity's specific enemies
restrictions: Cannot reach high levels of spell power (max 5th level)
Must actively crusade for deity or lose abilities

monk.description: Optional class from Oriental Adventures, usable in standard settings with DM permission. Discipline-focused unarmed combatants.
monk.source: Oriental Adventures / Complete Fighter's Handbook variant
monk.weapons: Unarmed; hand weapons; simple weapons only

special_abilities_by_level.level_1: Unarmed damage 1d4; AC improves to base 10 (no DEX penalty from armor)
special_abilities_by_level.level_3: Slowfall (take no damage from falls up to 20 ft)
special_abilities_by_level.level_7: Speak with animals (free, limited to one type)
special_abilities_by_level.level_9: Unarmed damage 1d8; ki focus: bonus attack per round
special_abilities_by_level.level_11: Mind blank (immune to mind-reading) at will

ki_points.description: Monks accumulate ki points representing spiritual energy. Ki points are used for special abilities.

multiclass_options.description: Additional multiclass combinations allowed for expanded races

allowed_combinations.notes: Very rare; requires exceptional INT and WIS

multiclass_options.dual_class_note: Human dual-class characters can switch from any standard class to these additional classes following normal dual-class rules
